sourcesContent:
import React from 'react'; import './Projects.css'; const projects = [ { number: '01', title: 'MovieMate', subtitle: 'Conversational AI Recommender', description: 'A context-aware movie recommendation chatbot using FAISS vector search, sentence-transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2), and a Groq-hosted LLaMA 3.3 70B backend. Dataset assembled via OMDb API, BeautifulSoup scraping, and multi-source merging. Deployed with a Streamlit frontend.', tags: ['Python', 'FAISS', 'LLaMA 3.3', 'Streamlit', 'NLP'], github: 'https://github.com/arushikhethavath/MovieMate', }, { number: '02', title: 'Predictive Maintenance', subtitle: 'NASA CMAPSS Dataset', description: 'Applied KMeans clustering on the NASA CMAPSS turbofan engine dataset to detect anomalous degradation patterns and predict remaining useful life. Used sensor fusion and dimensionality reduction to surface failure signals before breakdown.', tags: ['Python', 'KMeans', 'scikit-learn', 'ML', 'Data Science'], github: 'https://github.com/arushikhethavath', }, { number: '03', title: 'Secure TCP Chat', subtitle: 'Network Security Lab', description: 'Implemented a password-authenticated multi-client TCP chat application in C with XOR-based message encryption. Migrated from Linux headers to Winsock2 API to ensure cross-platform Windows compilation.', tags: ['C', 'Winsock2', 'TCP/IP', 'Encryption', 'Networking'], github: 'https://github.com/arushikhethavath', }, { number: '04', title: 'Data Warehouse Analytics', subtitle: 'Big Data Course Project', description: 'Designed star and snowflake schema data warehouses, wrote complex OLAP queries covering all five classical operations (drill-down, roll-up, slice, dice, pivot), and implemented advanced windowed SQL functions including RANK() with PARTITION BY.', tags: ['SQL', 'OLAP', 'Data Warehousing', 'Big Data'], github: 'https://github.com/arushikhethavath', }, ]; function Projects() { return (
Projects

Things I've
Built

A selection of hands-on engineering work — from ML research to full-stack applications.

{projects.map((p) => (
{p.number}

{p.title}

{p.subtitle}

View on GitHub

{p.description}

{p.tags.map((t, i) => ( {t} ))}
))}
); } export default Projects;
